prompt: The floating-point comparison fails unexpectedly. What is the bug?
choices: double addition is not defined for 0.1 and 0.2
Floating-point arithmetic is imprecise; 0.1+0.2 is not exactly 0.3 in binary, so == comparison fails
a should be declared as float, not double
The if condition should use a.compareTo(0.3) == 0
explanation: 0.1 and 0.2 have no exact binary representations. Their sum (≈0.30000000000000004) is not == 0.3. Fix: use an epsilon comparison: Math.abs(a - 0.3) < 1e-9, or use BigDecimal for exact arithmetic.
